Ad Text:
Emanate Technology is working with one of their key partners to identify a skilled and experience Strategic Writer.   A Canberra-based consultancy delivering expertise to the market across Management Advisory, Capability Management, and Information Technology Services. This organisation is a trusted strategic advisor and proven delivery partner, with an excellent reputation for solving their clients’ most challenging business problems.  Their people are pragmatic, energetic, and passionate professionals who bring diverse life experiences to high-performing teams. Filling your Work, Live and Reward buckets is an important part of what makes them a great place to be. Empowered by workforce diversity and encourage applications from all ages, nationalities, abilities, and cultures.

We are seeking seasoned Strategic Writers who have strong analytical and critical thinking skills as well as excellent oral and written communication skills. You will be able to master the new subject matter quickly, identify key issues, analyse problems, and communicate clearly
your views and reasoning.
 
We work in highly sensitive areas so discretion is paramount. This is a full-time client-facing role. 5 days on site. 
 
Expectations:



Assist in the creation of capability proposals, including business cases, cabinet submissions, and Ministerial briefs.
Guide these proposals through the internal committee process for Government consideration.
Analyse project documentation to identify weaknesses in the business case.
Stay informed on government policy and direction to craft a persuasive narrative supporting proposal approval.
Develop and maintain relationships with project teams, senior executives, internal stakeholders, and central agencies to garner support for the proposals
 
We are looking for strong candidates who have :



Strong writing skills – produce clear, concise, accurate, and timely documentation for cabinet consideration.
Skilled in analysing and interpreting complex issues to develop compelling business cases.
Proficient in persuasion and influence through strong engagement and collaboration skills.
Strategic in managing issues, applying expertise and innovative thinking.
Systematic planner, delivering quality products within agreed timeframes.
Proactive in identifying and resolving issues in consultation with key stakeholders.
Capable of building positive working relationships with stakeholders while managing competing agendas.
Effective in managing pressure and coping with setbacks.
 
Education/Qualifications



Must have Active AGSVA Negative Vetting 1 (minimum)
Undergraduate and/or post-graduate degree qualifications, or equivalent work experience recognised by the firm

If you hold an NV1 clearance and brings experience across what is outlined above then apply today!

Principal Economist/Policy Analyst, Federal Financial Relations

·       Employment Type: Ongoing

·       Classification / Grade / Band: Clerk Grade 11/12

·       Remuneration: $149,736- $173,174; plus, Superannuation and Leave Loading

·       Location: Sydney CBD, Flexible working options available

·       Applications Close: Tuesday, 14 April 2026 at 9:59am




Role impact & contribution

As a Principal Adviser in the Federal Financial Relations team, your day will involve shaping high‑quality economic and policy advice that supports NSW priorities. You’ll lead analysis on complex revenue, tax and intergovernmental issues, working closely with colleagues across Treasury and engaging with key external stakeholders.

You’ll monitor emerging policy developments, identify risks and opportunities, and translate complex issues into clear, evidence‑based advice for senior executives and Ministers. A typical day may include drafting or reviewing briefs, coordinating input across teams, and contributing to strategic discussions on national reform.

You’ll also guide and mentor junior staff, balancing workloads and supporting their development to deliver timely, high‑quality outcomes. Alongside this, you’ll contribute to broader branch and divisional priorities, helping to ensure advice is proactive, collaborative and aligned with the Government’s reform agenda.

You will be joining our Economic Strategy & Productivity Group and will be working within the Revenue Division. Read more about your future Group and Division.




What you can bring to the role.

In this integral role there are a few things we would like for you to have to best support yourself and add value to the people of NSW.

The ideal candidate should meet the following criteria:

·       Relevant tertiary qualifications (e.g. Economics, Statistics, Public Policy, Law) or equivalent experience

·       Proven experience delivering strategic policy, commercial advice or economic analysis in a data‑driven environment

·       Strong leadership capability, with experience guiding teams to analyse complex data and policy issues

·       Ability to apply critical thinking across diverse and sensitive policy matters to support sound advice and forecasts

·       Advanced communication skills, able to clearly explain complex information to senior leaders and Ministers

 

What can you expect from us

The team you will join is made up of a diverse and welcoming group of people. We understand what starting a new job is like, and to ensure you have the most positive experience we will ‘Buddy’ you up with a team member to help settle you in.

Just some of the benefits of working with Treasury include:

·       Flexible work at our Sydney office with 35-hour standard weeks

·       Accrual of flex time for extra hours worked, promoting work-life balance.

·       Ongoing learning opportunities through expert-led sessions and online training

·       Wellbeing programs to support your mental and physical health.

·       Various leave options, including paid parental and carers leave.

Read more about our benefits and how we invest in you.

 

Enrich your career with the NSW Treasury

You will have an invaluable opportunity to create a positive impact for the people of New South Wales. You will be challenged to provide the best outcomes possible in this role while developing and growing your own skills.

When you join Treasury, you join an organisation that is a whole-of-government financial and economic advisor. Treasury is a critical component of the government and provides advice that informs a wide range of financial, economic and policy driven work for the state of NSW.

As you contribute your expertise, you’ll engage in continuous Professional Development, acquiring essential skills such as:

·       Build capability to deliver high‑quality work under pressure, managing tight deadlines with confidence

·       Develop skills to navigate complex, ambiguous policy environments and resolve issues with incomplete information

·       Strengthen stakeholder management and influencing skills while advocating Treasury positions on sensitive and contested issues

Gather more insights about Careers at Treasury and learn more about how you can transform your career in a central government agency invested in you.

 

Interested in working with us

Read the role description to learn more about the role and submit your application ONLY via the I Work for NSW jobs platform.


Application process:

·       an up-to-date resume detailing your knowledge, skills, and experience in relation to the role.

·       complete pre-screening questions as part of the application process.

·       partake in a behavioural based interview and undergo additional assessments if shortlisted

A talent pool may be created for ongoing and temporary opportunities in the same role or grade that may become available over the next 18 months.

Read more about what to expect in the hiring process.

 

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

NSW Treasury is committed to building a workforce that reflects the diverse communities we serve, where everyone feels welcome, supported and able to contribute and do their best work.

We are committed to fair and equitable recruitment processes and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, including First Nations peoples, people with disability, people of all ages and genders, LGBTQIA+ people, and people from diverse cultural, linguistic and religious backgrounds.

We actively support workplace and recruitment adjustments to remove barriers and enable equitable participation. If you require an adjustment or assistance at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact the Talent Advisor listed on the job advertisement or visit the following link for more information: https://www.nsw.gov.au/departments-and-agencies/nsw-treasury/about-us/careers

·       As a Disability Confident Recruiter, NSW Treasury is committed to inclusive and accessible recruitment and supports adjustments so candidates with disability can participate fairly at every stage.

·       As a Level 1 Activate Carer Employer, we recognise and support employees with caring responsibilities through inclusive and flexible workplace practices.

 

Eligibility
To be eligible to apply for ongoing employment at NSW Treasury, you must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen or permanent resident of Australia.

NSW Treasury does not provide relocation expenses, nor can we support employer-sponsored work visas unless specifically referenced in the advertisement.

The successful candidate will be required to undergo background checks and provide confirmation of tertiary academic qualifications before commencement.

Any questions?

Visit our Careers Centre or contact our Talent Advisor Natalie Wu natallie.wu@treasury.nsw.gov.au.

Head of Business Development

Location: Burwood East, VIC
Reporting to: Chief of International & Australia Programs
Role Type: Full time, permanent

Lead at Scale. Deliver Impact That Lasts.

At World Vision Australia, we work in nearly 100 countries alongside communities, governments, and partners to create lasting change for children. This is a senior enterprise leadership role where strategy, stewardship, and purpose come together. You will shape how sustainable revenue is generated, ethically, effectively, and at scale across complex markets and funding environments. As part of an experienced leadership group, your decisions will influence how resources are mobilised and directed globally, delivering meaningful, measurable impact for children and communities.

About the Role

You will lead and evolve Business Development as an integrated organisational capability—bringing together acquisition, program quality, and diversified revenue into a cohesive, high-performing system. This role is central to strengthening market position, expanding funding pathways, and ensuring long-term sustainability without compromising values, ownership, or impact.

Top Priorities

Set Direction: Define and drive a clear Business Development strategy aligned to mission, markets, and long-term growth.
Integrate for Performance: Build a unified system across acquisitions, program quality, and diversified revenue that delivers results.
Steward Revenue & Risk: Optimise the portfolio to balance growth, sustainability, and organisational capacity.
Strengthen Market Position: Enhance competitiveness and funding success through strong positioning, partnerships, and program quality.
Lead & Influence: Develop senior leaders and shape enterprise outcomes through strong executive and stakeholder engagement.
Drive Sustainable Impact: Ensure revenue strategies translate into ethical, high-impact outcomes for children and communities.

About You

You are a strategic, values-driven executive with deep experience leading complex revenue or business development systems.

You bring:

Proven success leading large-scale business development, partnerships, or funding portfolios
Strong strategic judgement, balancing growth, risk, and impact
Experience leading and developing senior leaders
Exceptional stakeholder engagement across complex, matrixed environments
High integrity, accountability, and emotional intelligence
Alignment with World Vision Australia’s mission and values

Experience within international development, humanitarian, or similarly complex sectors is highly regarded.

The Selection Process

For your information, the interviews will start immediately, and the role may be filled before the closing date.

We embrace diversity, employing people from a variety of cultural and religious backgrounds. We take our Safeguarding responsibilities seriously and we are committed to providing an environment that is safe for children. Our stringent recruitment procedures make sure the safest and most suitable people work with the children in our programs. All successful candidates will undergo all required pre-employment checks which is inclusive of a criminal record check. We provide our staff and volunteers with ongoing supervision, support, and training in their work with vulnerable children and their families. World Vision Australia participates in the https://misconduct-disclosure-scheme.org/ for the Disclosure of Safeguarding related Misconduct in Recruitment Process within the Humanitarian and Development Sector.

Who we are

World Vision is a Christian organisation that empowers everyday Australians to create meaningful change for children through relief, development and advocacy work. For more information on World Vision and the work we do, please visit our website:
https://www.worldvision.com.au/about-us

Ready to Apply?

If you are a senior leader seeking to apply your strategic expertise at scale, in a role where governance, impact, and purpose matter, we invite you to apply.

About the role

We’re looking for an Associate Director / Director to join a high-performing data and technology consulting team, to deliver complex, large-scale programs across enterprise environments.

This role is a balance of technical depth, strong client engagement and team leadership. You’ll work closely with enterprise clients to solve complex data challenges, lead delivery across multiple engagements and mentor teams, while still staying close enough to the tech to guide architecture and solution design.

You’ll be someone who can confidently sit in front of senior stakeholders, translate technical concepts into business outcomes and lead teams to deliver high-quality results.

Permanent Full Time

Sydney based


Responsibilities

Lead delivery of data and analytics solutions across complex programs

Use tools such as Databricks, PySpark, SQL and Azure to analyse large datasets

Design and implement data-driven solutions to support business outcomes

Manage multiple engagements and ensure high quality delivery within timelines

Work closely with stakeholders to interpret data and provide insights

Mentor and support junior team members through coaching and development

Contribute to the development and deployment of new analytics tools and capabilities

Stay across emerging technologies and bring new ideas into the team




About you

7+ years experience working with data analytics and cloud technologies

Strong hands-on experience with Databricks, PySpark and SQL

Experience working with Azure cloud environments

Exposure to AI or advanced analytics tools is highly regarded

Proven ability to lead projects and manage stakeholders

Strong communication skills with the ability to translate data into insights

Experience working in Big4 consulting or large enterprise environments

Passion for technology, innovation and continuous learning

Completed a Bachelor in IT, or equivalent (preferred)




Benefits

Flexible working model with hybrid setup and genuine work from anywhere approach

Access to large-scale, high-profile clients and complex programs that accelerate your career

Continuous learning and development with structured training, certifications and clear progression pathways

Discounts and perks across travel, dining, retail and entertainment through internal programs

Strong wellbeing support including mental health programs and access to health initiatives

26 weeks paid parental leave with no tenure requirement, plus strong family-friendly policies




How to apply

If you are a senior data analytics professional with the aforementioned skills please hit APPLY or reach out to our team for a confidential discussion alyssa@assemblesolutions.com.au

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact

As an Associate Data Scientist at AMP, you'll play a hands‑on role in turning data into insight that genuinely informs decision‑making across the organisation. Working within the Data Platforms team, you'll support the development of analytical and data science solutions that help teams better understand customers, performance, and risk. Through close collaboration with Data Engineers, Reporting & Insights specialists, and data governance partners, you'll help ensure AMP's enterprise data is analysed thoughtfully, responsibly, and with purpose.

This role offers meaningful exposure to real‑world analytics and modelling use cases. You'll contribute to exploratory data analysis, assist in building and validating statistical and machine‑learning models, and help translate analytical outputs into clear, usable insights. Your work will directly support high‑quality reporting and analytics outcomes, while building the foundational skills that underpin AMP's growing data science capability.

What you'll bring to the role

You'll bring a strong analytical mindset and a genuine curiosity about how data can be used to solve business problems. With experience working with structured datasets in enterprise environments, you're comfortable using SQL to explore and analyse data, and Python or R to support modelling and feature engineering. You understand the importance of data quality, governance, and documentation, and you approach analysis with care, rigour, and attention to detail.

Equally important is your ability to collaborate and communicate. You're able to explain analytical findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders, and you enjoy working as part of a cross‑functional team. With experience in Agile or iterative delivery environments, you're keen to keep learning, take feedback on board, and continue developing your data science capability in a practical, applied setting.

You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

Company Description

AVEC is a leading mid-market technology consultancy that's redefining the art of technology consulting with a human touch. Our approach is simple, smart, and refreshingly straightforward. In just 5 years, we've grown to over $50 million in revenue, and we are only getting started.

As part of the $1 billion revenue Talent International Group AVEC stands tall within this largest privately owned and independent group in our sector. Talent is celebrated for its innovative thinking and a world-class culture.

Role location: Based in Australia across various states 

Job Description

Job purpose:

The Senior Architect – AI & Automation is a client-facing role responsible for shaping, designing, and delivering AI and automation solutions that drive measurable business outcomes for clients. This role partners closely with clients to understand their strategic priorities, identify opportunities, and architect scalable, innovative solutions that enhance efficiency, insight, and competitive advantage.

Key roles and responsibilities:

The key roles and responsibilities of the role include:

Client Advisory & Strategy

Act as a trusted advisor to clients on Architecture, AI and automation strategy, use cases, and roadmap development.
Engage with senior client stakeholders to understand business challenges and translate them into technology-enabled solutions.
Identify and shape opportunities to embed AI and automation into client operating models.
Provide thought leadership on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices.
Advisory, Solution Architecture, Consulting Delivery and/or Program and Project Management across key Programs and Projects. (85% Utilisation)

Solution Architecture & Design

Understand Avec Engagement module, methodologies and Delivery enablers.
Lead the design of end-to-end AI and automation solutions in conjunction with practice leaders, tailored to client needs.
Develop scalable, secure, and commercially viable architectures across AI, machine learning, and intelligent automation.
Ensure alignment with client enterprise architecture, data environments, and technology ecosystems.
Define reusable patterns, frameworks, and accelerators to support consistent delivery.

Delivery & Implementation Oversight

Provide architectural leadership across the full lifecycle of client engagements—from discovery through to deployment.
Partner with delivery teams to ensure solutions are implemented in line with design, quality, and client expectations.
Support estimation, planning, and solution scoping during pre-sales and delivery phases.
Troubleshoot complex technical challenges and guide teams to resolution.

Commercial & Pre-Sales Contribution

Support business development activities, including proposals, RFP responses, and client presentations.
Shape solution narratives that clearly articulate business value, ROI, and outcomes.
Contribute to building a strong pipeline of AI and automation opportunities.
Partner with sales and account teams to position differentiated offerings.

Data, AI & Automation Enablement

Ensure robust data strategies underpin all AI solutions, including data quality, governance, and integration.
Guide the development and deployment of machine learning models and automation workflows.
Promote responsible, ethical, and compliant use of AI technologies across client solutions.

Stakeholder Engagement

Build strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders at all levels.
Communicate complex technical concepts in a clear, compelling, and business-focused way.
Influence decision-making and drive alignment across client and delivery teams.
Ensure a constructive working environment withing which the team can grow and excel.

Capability & Practice Development

Contribute to the growth of AI and automation capability within the organisation.
Mentor team members and share knowledge across projects.
Support the development of reusable assets, methodologies, and best practices.
Qualifications

Key Requirements:

To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate:

Technical Expertise

Strong experience in AI/ML, automation (RPA), and intelligent automation solutions.
Agile Practitioner
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP) and AI services.
Solid understanding of data architecture, APIs, and system integration.
Hands-on experience with tools such as Python, Power Platform, UiPath, or similar.

Architecture & Consulting

Proven experience in a Senior Architect, Solution Architect, or Consulting role .
Experience designing and delivering enterprise-scale solutions for clients.
Strong understanding of architecture frameworks and delivery methodologies.

Commercial & Client Engagement

Demonstrated experience in client-facing roles with strong stakeholder management.
Experience supporting pre-sales, proposals, and commercial engagements.
Ability to articulate value and translate technical solutions into business outcomes.

Key Behaviours

Client-centric and commercially astute
Strategic thinker with strong problem-solving capability
Influential communicator and trusted advisor
Innovative, adaptable, and outcome-focused
Collaborative and delivery-oriented
Excellent relationship management skills to build, maintain and foster strong relationships with customers, partners, and internal teams.
Can independently lead module streams workshops, design and configuration.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
Effective time management skills to handle multiple tasks and projects simultaneously.

Work Health and Safety 

Follow safe work practices and perform all duties in a manner that ensures personal health and safety and the safety of other employees, visitors and guests in the workplace.
Comply with Talent International’s work, health and safety management system, policies and procedures.

Values

Culture is integral to our organisation and its success. It is therefore important

that you align with our core values.

Lead the way - We have a passionate desire to carve a unique path in our field. Free of the restraints and agendas of the large consultancy firms, we don't restrict ourselves to one way of thinking and are constantly looking for new ways to deliver.
Strive for better - We are determined to challenge the status quo and will always look to raise the industry standard. If there’s a better way of doing things, we will endeavour to find it.
Give a damn - Trust is paramount. We have a deep-rooted care for our clients and treat their business on a very personal level. We expect transparency and integrity. Furthermore, people and their well-being are at the heart of all that we do.

Award-winning workplace culture
Industry leading parental leave policy
Extremely attractive and competitive bonus scheme
Annual 'TALENTFEST' conferences
Equity program (everyone gets a piece of the pie!)
Global mobility
Opportunities for development and career progression

Experience using Atlassian tools such as Jira and Confluence for delivery tracking and documentation.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

 

About the team

As the way Australians shop continues to evolve, our Data and AI Team keep Coles at the forefront of modern retail. We combine data, artificial intelligence, and applied engineering to reimagine how our stores, supply chain, and digital channels operate — creating smarter, more connected, and seamless experiences for our customers and team members.

 

Operating as a centralised development and enablement function within Coles Technology, the team unites product, engineering, and platform capabilities to deliver the intelligence layer of the enterprise. We serve every part of Coles — from computer vision applications that enhance in-store experiences and reduce loss, to forecasting, optimisation, and decision-support solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy, and agility across our supply chain, retail operations, and corporate functions.

 

The scale and impact of this work are significant. Coles serves millions of customers each week across more than a thousand stores nationwide, and our AI and data capabilities provide the intelligence that underpins how we plan, move, sell, and serve. By combining deep data capability with applied AI and modern engineering, we’re enabling Coles to become a truly AI-enabled enterprise, transforming how we operate and how Australia shops every day.

 



About the role

 

We are currently recruiting for a Principal Engineer who will sit within the Data & AI Technology Hub and leads the design and build of enterprise measurement, experimentation, and causal inference solutions deployed across Coles’ business domains. Working in a hub-and-spoke model, the role partners with domain teams including Retail Media, Performance Marketing, Promo Effectiveness, and other commercial, digital, and operational functions to improve how Coles designs experiments, quantifies incremental impact, and makes investment decisions.

 

 

Key responsibilities will include:

 

 

Define the frameworks, standards, and roadmap to establish Coles measurement capability, and work with teams in Data & AI to apply the measurement capability and reduce fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Provide enterprise thought leadership on design, methodology, and decision quality.
Research, evaluate, and champion adoption of modern measurement methodologies including Bayesian inference, causal modelling (DAGs, difference-in-differences, synthetic controls), advanced A/B test design, uplift modelling, and multi-touch attribution frameworks. Success in this area is defined by reusable experimentation and measurement frameworks being adopted across multiple business domains, and a measurable reduction in fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Define and own measurement and experimentation standards and capabilities across Data & AI, including test design, power analysis, metric definition, and validation frameworks, driving improved rigor and consistency in how Coles designs experiments. Champion measurement excellence by setting the bar for analytical rigour, influencing architecture decisions for experimentation platforms, uplifting quantitative capability and participating in experiment and measurement design reviews.
Provide deep technical knowledge and hands-on leadership across engineering squads, guiding the design of experiments, attribution models, and business case measurement approaches. Act as the go-to expert brought into projects to advise on measurement methodology, audience/cohort definition, control group design, and causal inference strategy, enabling faster, more credible ROI assessment for interventions and campaigns
Influence and mentor data scientists, analysts, and engineers across multiple squads in measurement methodology, raising the organisation's quantitative literacy and analytical maturity
Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with business partners, domain leadership, and cross-domain product teams. Be recognised as the person who makes complex measurement accessible and actionable for non-technical audiences.
Partner with marketing, commercial, and product leadership to embed rigorous measurement into campaign evaluation, use case design, and business case validation. Ensure consistent measurement methodology across Data & AI while delivering compliant, transparent, and trustworthy analytical outcomes.

 

 

 

About you and your skills

 

 

Experience working effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment
Experience operating as a senior measurement specialist in a fast-paced, complex environment, balancing deep-focus project work with, research and ad-hoc advisory across multiple teams
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams (e.g. marketing, product, commercial, finance) to design and deliver measurement solutions that directly inform investment decisions and strategic priorities.
Proven ability to bring together diverse stakeholders (marketing, commercial, product, engineering) and align them on measurement approach, success criteria, and interpretation of results, including experience coaching analysts, data scientists, and business partners to think critically about measurement, ask the right questions, and interpret quantitative evidence correctly
Demonstrated ownership of end-to-end measurement delivery: from problem framing and experiment design through execution, analysis, and presentation of findings to senior leadership.
Track record of delivering production grade analytical systems, through individual framework development to full solution design and implementation
Proven experience designing and scaling advanced measurement systems: A/B and multivariate testing platforms, causal inference pipelines, attribution models, and business case validation frameworks that balance statistical rigour with practical delivery timelines
Expertise with modern data science and engineering toolsets including CI/CD (through DevOps/MLOps/DataOps), experimentation platforms, automated testing, and infrastructure-as-code.
Demonstrated experience of influencing solution design to enable robust and reliable insight generation, evaluation and experimentation.

 

 

Qualifications

 

 

Postgraduate qualification in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Econometrics, Bioinformatics or a related measurement science discipline. Post-doctoral or equivalent industry experience in applied measurement/causal inference highly regarded.
Ability to set measurement strategy in the context of Coles' broader direction, anticipating where the organisation needs measurement capability and proactively building the frameworks, tools, and knowledge to get there. Ability to balance statistical perfectionism with commercial pragmatism: knowing when a quick directional read is sufficient vs. when full causal rigour is required.
Expert-level knowledge of experimental design (A/B, multivariate, switchback, geo-experiments), causal inference (propensity scores, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences), Bayesian modelling, and Marketing Mixture Models (MMM).
Highly experienced in Python and/or R for statistical computing, with deep proficiency in causal inference libraries (eg DoWhy, CausalImpact, EconML), Bayesian frameworks (eg PyMC, Stan, NumPyro), and experiment analysis tooling
Familiarity with machine learning frameworks (PyTorch, scikit-learn), feature-flag systems (LaunchDarkly, Optimizely) and analytics platforms (Adobe, Google Analytics)
Strong architectural thinking for measurement systems: able to design scalable experimentation platforms, metric stores, and attribution pipelines
Ability to translate new research into practical business applications within the Coles context
Strong understanding of data privacy and protection principles as they apply to experimentation (e.g. PII in test/control groups, consent, data retention).
Outstanding communicator and storyteller: able to present measurement findings, methodology rationale, and strategic recommendations to audiences ranging from data scientists to the executive leadership team.

 

 

 



What’s in it for you?

 

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.

Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

Cathara Consulting is an Australian-based Consulting firm specialising in Business Transformations, Technology Consulting & Resourcing Services.

Our Darwin based client requires a Technical Business Analyst to work within the ICT projects space. This will be an initial 12 month contract, with strong extension options.

You must be willing to relocate to Darwin and remain there for the duration of the contract. No remote working options are available for this opportunity.

The role

You will be working on a large multi-year program to uplift our clients ICT environment to support the implementation of a new solution including the consolidation of data and reporting facilities from several systems.

Key Duties and Responsibilities




Facilitate workshops, interviews and focus groups to elicit requirements from project stakeholders, including the software vendor.

Responsible for understanding business needs, collecting data, delivering analysis and identifying, evaluating and recommending solution options and preparation of quality evidence-based written reports and other documents, including future state data and process flow maps.

Develop standardised and consistent technical specification documents for technical domains, including reporting, data migration, and system integrations that supports effective quality assurance and testing.

Establish information management business and functional requirements.

Conduct walkthroughs to validate and obtain approval of business analysis outputs and work packages.

Work closely with project / business stakeholders and vendor to communicate technical requirements, impacts, technical deliverables, vendor related dependencies ensure alignment with business objectives.

Work with solution architects, technical leads, developers and vendors to develop customer centric solutions.

Analyse new source systems and reference data needed for codification and assist with development and configuration activities.

Assist in the implementation of best practices around the Development, Process, and System Integration and Testing.

Collaborate with the testing team to design and implement validation processes that ensure technical requirements are met.

Essential Skills and Experience




Hands on experience in technical analysis and capturing clear technical specifications for data, integrations, reporting, and system configuration.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organise, analyse and disseminate significant amounts of information across project teams and stakeholders with attention to detail and accuracy.

Experience in large transformational project/program management within established structures and the ability to work to tight deadlines and competing priorities.

Experience communicating with a wide range of stakeholders including project team members, technical staff, subject matter experts, vendors and executive.

Vendor liaison skills to effectively communicate between internal teams and vendor representatives, manage dependencies, and interpret vendor solution capabilities.

Data and integration expertise, including understanding data structures, mappings, migration, APIs, and interactions across legacy and vendor systems.

Impact analysis and problem solving to assess technical impacts across multiple systems, identify gaps, risks, and solution options, and ensure alignment with business needs.

Knowledge of Microsoft SQL server development and experience using T-SQL, extract-transform-load (ETL) processes, data quality analysis and associated tools such as SSIS and Microsoft Data Quality Services.

Provide BA and system support, relevant to provision of requirements support and clarification to design, build, test, implementation and Business As Usual support activities.


Desirable Skills and Experience




Hands on experience working with systems replacement project with COTS product.

Exposure to release management i.e. build upgrades, change requests, impact assessment, and release processes, ensuring alignment with technical deliveries.

Knowledge of data architecture / design concepts and techniques.

Experience in an education-IT-related field, project or software or other relevant education subject-matter knowledge.

Tertiary qualifications in information technology, computer science or software engineering or Business Analysis Certification, or equivalent.

Experience using Atlassian tools such as Jira and Confluence.

About Guild Insurance and Acerta 

At Guild Insurance, our people feel genuinely connected to our purpose. We are focused on the future, thrive on challenge and learn from one another. We care deeply about our customers and each other, making a real difference by supporting the people and professions our communities rely on. At Guild Insurance we thrive together.

Acerta is Guild Insurance’s intermediary division, delivering insurance solutions supported by an experienced team providing timely broking advice and professional insight. As we invest further in digital capability, this is an exciting time to join Acerta and help shape how brokers engage with us digitally. 

 

The Opportunity 

The Digital Lead – Intermediary leads and shapes the digital experience for Acerta’s broker partners. Acting as the key link between brokers, underwriting teams and internal digital stakeholders, this role ensures our intermediary platforms perform reliably today and continue to evolve in line with business strategy. 

Sitting at the intersection of brokers, underwriting and digital delivery, you will combine hands-on operational involvement with strategic influence. Using broker insight and data, you will help prioritise improvements that enhance usability, efficiency and engagement across the intermediary channel.

The role provides advanced financial modelling, performance analysis, budgeting insight and activity-based evaluation to support strategic and operational decision-making across the Peninsula Care Group.

Working within PARU, the role strengthens organisational financial sustainability through high-level analytical expertise, enhanced reporting frameworks and robust planning tools. The position collaborates closely with Finance Business Partners, Activity Based Funding and Activity Based Costing team, Financial Reporting and operational leaders, to deliver accurate, timely and actionable financial insights that support organisational performance, accountability and continuous improvement.

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world's leading independently-owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms.

Our culture is underpinned by a commitment to our clients, people and communities, and our promise to ‘Reach for Remarkable’ by providing proven expertise, principled execution, and personalised experience.

In Australia, we’re proud to support the careers of over 1,500 remarkable people. With six offices nationally and partner firms in more than 145 markets, wherever you want to go, we can take you there.

About this role

Join Grant Thornton’s growing Outsourced CFO Services team, as a Senior Manager, part of our national Private Enterprise practice. We partner with dynamic, mid‑market clients to elevate their finance capability, deliver commercial insights, and drive business performance.

You’ll work closely with senior leaders including our national Outsourced CFO Partner group, who advise clients across sectors on strategic finance uplift, board reporting, cashflow optimisation, financial modelling, and end‑to‑end finance transformation. Recent engagements include designing scalable finance functions for high‑growth businesses, implementing cloud‑based finance systems, and providing CFO‑level insight to founders and boards.

This is a key growth role in an established but expanding service offering, based in Sydney or Melbourne.

Key responsibilities

Lead client finance strategy and deliver CFO‑level advice
Shape finance functions and design scalable operating models
Present insights to boards, owners and C‑suite
Build commercial dashboards and drive data‑led decisions
Guide finance system design and transformation
Strengthen client relationships and identify growth opportunities
Support business development and expand service offering

Skills & experience

Previous experience in professional services, ideally within Business Services, Audit, or Consulting
Commercial finance or Finance Business Partner experience in a commercial setting
Confident presenting to boards, CFOs and business owners
Strong tech capability and influence across stakeholders
Leadership and oversight of finance professionals
Degree in accounting/finance; CA or CPA qualified

Reach your remarkable

Overview

FinXL IT Professional Services is a leading IT consultancy that partners with clients to bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. Since 2003, we've been delivering high-value ICT services across financial services, telecommunications, government, and enterprise sectors.

We are seeking an experienced Financial Analyst or Consultant to provide hands-on support for a critical Fund Cost Analysis engagement. This is a Melbourne-based contract opportunity designed for a professional capable of delivering high-level insights within a complex environment.

Brennan. Where true performance thrives.

At Brennan, we believe that how technology is delivered is every bit as important as what the technology is. We focus on creating real and relevant value for customers with solutions that fit their specific needs and always reflect their true interests.
It’s a claim backed by our True Performance System - a way of working engineered to get us closer, and deliver better, for our customers and their actual experience of technology.

Why work for Brennan?  

At Brennan, we aim to lead, not follow. One of the ways we do this is through an open diverse culture that values performance, where anyone in the team can bring new ideas to the table and see them thrive. Our people are empowered, considerate, supportive, trusting and accepting, they are the cornerstone of the business. Because of this approach, we have become the largest Australian owned systems integrator in Australia and continue to grow.  
 
Brennan offers an excellent remuneration package and benefits including:  

A focus on health and well-being - social club, sporting teams, health checks, poker, trivia nights and more!  
A strong culture underpinned by values that are truly lived every day

High levels of reward and recognition for overachieving 
The Senior Project Manager (SPM) is accountable for the successful end to end delivery of complex digital and cloud projects within a professional services environment. The role is responsible for driving predictable project outcomes across scope, schedule, cost, quality and customer satisfaction while ensuring strong financial performance aligned to Brennan’s delivery and governance frameworks. 
  
The SPM operates as a senior delivery leader, trusted by executive and customer stakeholders, responsible for proactively managing risk, resolving delivery challenges, and ensuring cloud programs and projects are returned to plan when variance occurs. This role leads cross functional professional services delivery teams, comprising cloud, infrastructure and end user experience consultants, to deliver complex digital initiatives including cloud platforms, infrastructure modernisation, migration and transformation programs 
  
The SPM is a key contributor to ongoing continual improvement, providing thought leadership in delivery and governance capability and leveraging AI and technology to improve delivery productivity, quality of project outcomes and client experience.  

Please note this role can work from any of Brennan's Australian offices - Sydney, Melbourne, Canberra, Newcastle, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth or Hobart.
  
ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES 

Project Delivery Leadership, Control & Outcomes 

Lead a variety of concurrent professional services projects within the Infrastructure, Cloud and End User Experience Practice ranging from 1-2mth engagements through to complex, multi stream and phased roadmap of initiatives.  
Own accountability for delivery outcomes across assigned cloud and digital projects, ensuring contractual, commercial, and customer objectives are met.  
Establish clear delivery baselines for scope, schedule, budget and resources, and maintain disciplined governance throughout the project lifecycle.  
Proactively identify, assess and manage Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies and Decisions (RAIDD) throughout the project lifecycle. Maintaining high quality, auditable registers that provide transparency for internal governance and reporting. 
Drive mitigation strategies and contingency planning, escalating material risks early with clear options, trade-offs and recommendations.  
Enforce change control discipline across all projects, ensuring scope changes are formally assessed and approved.  
Lead change impact assessments, clearly articulating impacts to cost, schedule, risk, resourcing and commercials working with sales to convert to contractual variations. 
Plan and manage cross-functional project resources effectively ensuring alignment to critical path project milestones. 
Forecast resource demand and capacity requirements, identifying constraints and escalating risks early.  
Enable delivery teams through clear direction, prioritisation and decision making while fostering accountability and collaboration 
Act as the primary escalation point for delivery issues, ensuring timely decision making and resolution. 
Maintain excellent project information hygiene within PPM tool and associated project systems 
Financial & Commercial Management 

Take full ownership of project financial performance, including budget baselining, forecasting, earned value tracking, and margin protection.  
Actively monitor actuals against forecast and baseline, identifying variances early and implementing corrective actions.  
Develop and execute back to green recovery plans for projects at risk, covering cost, schedule, scope and resourcing impacts.  
Ensure all billable effort is accurately captured and aligned to client agreements and billing milestones 
Stakeholder Engagement & Communication 

Manage senior client stakeholders with confidence, setting expectations clearly and communicating impacts, trade-offs and decisions transparently.  
Lead project working group, client status meetings and sponsor level steering forums.  
Produce timely and concise, insight led project reporting that focuses on outcomes, risks, financial position and forward-looking forecasts.  
Navigate complex stakeholder environments, resolving conflict and aligning diverse priorities to project outcomes. 
On occasion, provide Project Management as a Service (PMaaS) within customer environments. 
PreSales Support and Initiation 

Partner with Practice leaders during presales, supporting proposal reviews, delivery approach validation, risk identification and project management estimation.  
Ensure smooth transition from sales to delivery with clear scope, assumptions, commercials, ensuring upfront client and delivery team alignment and establishing clear delivery baselines. 
Capability & Continuous Improvement 

Ensure adherence to Brennan’s Project Framework, delivery standards, and information security policies.  
Identify opportunities for delivery improvement and standardisation across delivery practices and actively contribute to maturity uplift initiatives.  
Maintain relevant industry certifications and demonstrate continual professional development. 
Key Competencies & Experience 

Delivery Experience 

Minimum 10 years' experience in Project Management and 5+ years at a senior level. 
Experience delivering digital or cloud projects within a Professional Services, IT consulting, or outsourced delivery environment. 
Experience operating within fast paced, high concurrent project volume environments within billable, margin driven delivery models. 
Professional Skills and Technology 

Proven track record of successful client implementations across relevant technologies: 
Microsoft Azure and AWS Cloud 
Microsoft Dynamics Business Central, Project Operations CRM 
IT infrastructure systems including identify, networking, data platforms, business reporting and security 
Relevant Project Management and Agile certifications (e.g. PMP, PRINCE2, Agile PM, SAFe) 
Strong proficiency with Microsoft Project and PPM tools. MS Project Operations preferred. 
Leadership & Behavioural Capabilities 

Confident, credible leader with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and executives. 
Highly proactive, outcomes driven and comfortable making decisions in complex environments. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate technical delivery detail into senior leadership level insights.  
Strong conflict resolution, escalation management and stakeholder negotiation skills 
Strong customer and team focus and role model of core values 
Coach and mentor for emerging and junior project professionals

The Company

Our client is a fast-growing, multi-brand consumer business operating within the FMCG/retail sector. With a strong footprint and continued expansion, they are investing in their commercial finance capability to better support performance and strategic decision-making across their brand portfolio.

The Role

Reporting to the Head of Commercial, this is a high-impact Commercial Finance Manager role partnering closely with senior stakeholders, including Brand CEOs. You will play a key role in driving profitability, delivering insights, and supporting strategic initiatives across multiple brands.

Lead budgeting, forecasting, and scenario modelling processes

Analyse performance across multiple brands and business units

Strong financial modelling and analytical capability
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
CA/CPA qualified (or equivalent)
Culture & Benefits

Broad exposure across multiple brands and senior stakeholders
Flexible working arrangements and supportive leadership
Genuine opportunity to influence commercial outcomes
How to Apply

This role acts as a conduit between the technical team and the key business stakeholders. The Opportunity

FourQuarters has been exclusively retained to appoint a Distribution Analyst on behalf of a leading Australian fund manager and non-bank lender. With a strong track record of performance and continued growth across its credit strategies, the business is investing in its distribution capability to support its next phase of expansion.

This role sits at the intersection of distribution, finance, and strategy, providing critical insights to drive origination, optimise channel performance, and support executive decision-making.

About the Role

Reporting to the Head of Distribution (with close alignment to Finance), the Distribution Analyst will play a key role in analysing performance across broker, intermediary, and direct channels. You will support the distribution team with data-driven insights, pipeline visibility, and commercial analysis to enhance decision-making and drive revenue outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse distribution performance across channels, including brokers, BDMs, and strategic partners
Track and report on origination volumes, conversion rates, and pipeline activity
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting packs for senior leadership
Provide insights on channel profitability and product performance
Support forecasting and scenario analysis for origination and revenue
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities across the distribution network

Assist in CRM (e.g. Salesforce) optimisation and reporting integrity
About You

Experience in an analytical or commercial role within financial services, funds management, banking, or lending
Strong analytical capability with advanced Excel skills; exposure to Power BI, Tableau, or SQL advantageous
Commercial mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Experience working with distribution, sales, or client-facing teams is highly regarded
Strong communication skills and ability to influence stakeholders
High attention to detail with a proactive and curious approach

Job description:



About the Opportunity:

We are looking for a Senior People Systems Analyst to join our Technology, Digital and Transformation team, with a focus on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. This role sits at the centre of our People and Culture systems landscape and plays a key part in ensuring our HR technology is reliable, optimised, and supporting the business effectively.

You will act as the functional expert across SuccessFactors, owning configuration, resolving complex issues, and driving continuous improvement across the employee lifecycle. Working closely with HR, payroll, and technology teams, you will ensure our people systems are accurate, compliant, and fit for purpose as the business evolves.

Configure and maintain SuccessFactors Employee Central including data models, workflows, business rules, and role-based permissions
Provide support across HCM modules such as Recruiting, Onboarding, Performance, Compensation, Learning, and Time Off
Apply creative, out‑of‑the‑box thinking to solution design, leveraging SuccessFactors in innovative ways to address complex people system challenges
Troubleshoot and resolve complex system and data issues across modules
Manage mass data changes and ensure strong data quality, validation, and governance practices
Maintain system security, access controls, and compliance with audit and privacy requirements
Support integrations and ensure reliable data flow across HR and payroll systems
Work closely with stakeholders across People and Culture, Technology, and external vendors

About You:

You will bring strong hands-on experience with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, with a deep understanding of configuration including data models, workflows, business rules, and security. You are confident managing complex system issues and enjoy getting into the detail to find the root cause and implement long term fixes.

You have experience working across multiple HCM modules and understand how data flows between systems, particularly in environments integrated with payroll. You are comfortable managing system releases, testing changes, and ensuring everything is delivered in a controlled and compliant way.

Experience with SuccessFactors certifications, mass data management, and process documentation will be highly regarded.


Why Join Us?

You will enjoy a range of great employee benefits and rewards including:

Competitive salary + bonus program
Enjoy additional yearly Well-Being and Community leave days
14-week paid parental leave, with equal benefit for both parents
Employee Referral Program
Employee Assistance Program - support and assistance for you and your partner at those times in life when you need it most
Maxxia Rewards - a great range of discounts and benefits at selected retail outlets, department stores, attractions, travel, cinemas, restaurants.
Discounts across the Australian Unity business including Private Health Insurance, General Insurance + more!
Available access to LinkedIn Learning courses through our great Learning platform

For further benefits visit: https://www.australianunity.com.au/careers/culture

Australian Unity is an Equal Opportunity employer and we encourage applications from all members of the community, including people of Aboriginal and Torres strait Islander descent, culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and, mature aged people.

Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced Data Platform Manager to lead a Data Engineering team and deliver robust, scalable analytics and data management solutions.

In this role, you will work closely with internal stakeholders and external partners to design, build, and maintain data pipelines, ensuring the platform is current, efficient, and cost effective. Your work will directly support decision making across operational and corporate areas, including operations, procurement, finance, and revenue cycle management.

You will also play a key role in improving engagement with business stakeholders, establishing standards for the data warehouse, and developing a framework for standardised reporting and dashboards.

Working with business subject matter experts and technology teams, you will implement best practices such as DataOps, Agile methodologies, and CI/CD, ensuring solutions are practical, timely, and easy to maintain.
 
Key Responsibilities

Lead the Data Engineering team, providing clear direction, coaching, and guidance
Deliver analytics and data platform solutions aligned with business requirements
Transition Power BI (PBIX) reports from multiple workspaces into a single, version-controlled workspace using Git and PBIP formats
Validate CI/CD pipelines for production code deployment, including semantic models (TMDL datasets), Power BI reports, and organisational apps
Consolidate multiple instances of patient management databases (webPAS) into unified tables to improve performance and efficiency
Monitor data pipeline runs and work proactively with analytics teams to reduce inefficiencies
Review and enhance GitHub Actions pipelines and refresh schedules to reduce complexity and inconsistencies
Support the consolidation of Snowflake databases into efficient ingestion patterns
Optimise platform operations, including alerting, compute usage, and workload management
Establish foundations for self-service analytics through improvements to the enterprise data warehouse
Lead the Data Engineering stream within a separation programme, ensuring effective delivery of pipelines, ETL processes, and data transfer mechanisms
Promote a safe and healthy workplace in line with organisational health and safety frameworks
Experience & Knowledge

Minimum of 5 years experience in a similar role
Proven experience in data architecture, modelling, testing frameworks, and data governance
Experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Snowflake, DBT)
Advanced understanding of dimensional data warehouse design and implementation
Strong knowledge of data engineering techniques
Experience with SQL performance tuning across multiple platforms
Must Haves

Proven experience leading Data Engineering teams and delivering scalable analytics
Strong expertise in cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Snowflake) and SQL tuning
Hands-on experience with CI/CD, DataOps, and standardised reporting
How to Apply

Click the “Apply” button below. If additional information is required reach out to Josh Tinton at JTinton@quinnallan.com.au

Company: Kingmill Pty Limited t/as SIXT Australia
Ad Text:
Company Description

At SIXT Australia, we're revolutionizing car rental. We believe it should be as exciting as a road trip – something to look forward to! We transform everyday mobility into memorable experiences with premium vehicles and service. It's in our DNA, blending Aussie spirit with German heritage – Das Ist Good. As part of the NRMA family, a trusted member-owned brand, we provide SIXT Star Service, ensuring every move is a memorable experience. 

Job Description

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Commercial Analyst to partner closely with our Operations, Fleet Management, Claims, Product and Property teams. In this high impact role, you’ll turn complex data into practical commercial insight that helps leaders make confident, timely decisions.

You’ll play a key role in delivering our Finance Plan through stronger forecasting, sharper reporting and smarter decision making - influencing outcomes across our national branch and fleet network.

This hybrid role is based in Sydney, giving you the flexibility to work from home and from our centrally located offices in Barrack Place and Sydney Olympic Park.

Your role at SIXT 

Your key responsibilities will include: 

Partner with operational leaders to understand performance drivers across fleet and branch operations, providing clear commercial insight to support decision‑making
Analyse key cost drivers (including fleet mix, damage and claims, and operational costs) to identify profitability and efficiency opportunities
Build robust business cases, track benefits, and complete post‑implementation reviews for operational initiatives
Deliver insightful reporting and dashboards (actuals vs forecast, trends and KPIs) to support weekly and monthly trading rhythms
Own budgeting and forecasting models, clearly articulating risks, opportunities and key drivers to stakeholders
Model and evaluate commission structures (including IRPD), providing scenario analysis and recommendations aligned to financial guardrails
Interpret commercial documents such as lease agreements, RFPs and tenders, identifying financial implications and risks
Translate analysis into clear, compelling recommendations and present insights to stakeholders at all levels
Build, automate and improve decision‑support tools using Excel (including Power Query) and BI / planning systems such as TM1, Tableau, Looker or NetSuite

Your skills matter

To be successful in the role, you will bring: 

5+ years’ experience in commercial finance, FP&A or a similar analytical role
CA or CPA qualification (or equivalent) — preferred
Advanced financial modelling and problem‑solving skills, with strong attention to detail
Excellent communication skills and the ability to turn data into a clear commercial story
Confidence working with ambiguity in a fast‑moving environment
Advanced Excel capability (including Power Query) and comfort working with large data sets
Experience with financial systems and BI tools (TM1, Tableau, Looker, NetSuite)

As part of our recruitment process, successful applicants will be required to complete pre-employment checks.

What we offer  

At SIXT we aren’t just about discounts (although you do get these too). We offer benefits to help make work and life just right for you!  

Progressive flexibility, leave and well-being benefits to balance all of life's priorities
Travel discounts on SIXT car rental, cruises, and accommodation at our award-winning NRMA Holiday Parks and Resorts.  
Complimentary myNRMA Rewards membership including free Roadside Assistance & discounts on groceries, movie tickets, gift cards, gym memberships, attractions, restaurants and much more  
Discounts on a range of NRMA personal insurance products including car, home & travel  
Grow, progress or relocate your career and move around the NRMA Group or different locations with us.  

We’re for inclusion, diversity, and representing the customers and communities we serve. That’s why we welcome applications from First Nations, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, people of all genders, members of the LGBTQI+ community, and anyone else who wants to be part of our team.  

Our Talent Acquisition Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. The NRMA is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Job Title: Principal – Credit & Risk Industry Benchmarking – Perpetual Digital
Ad Text:
Work with senior leaders across major financial institutions in a high-trust, peer-led environment

Enjoy up to six weeks of leave plus an annual wellbeing allowance to support your health and lifestyle

Hybrid working model with 2–3 work from home days

Modern, centrally located CBD offices with easy access to public transport

Perpetual Group is an ASX-listed company (ASX:PPT) headquartered in Sydney, Australia, consisting of 11 leading brands across three distinct businesses: Asset Management, Wealth Management and Corporate Trust.  

A specialist business within the Corporate Trust division, Perpetual Roundtables provides confidential, data‑driven benchmarking and executive peer forums that help financial institutions compare performance, identify risk trends, and make better strategic decisions across lending, risk and collections.

The business operates thirteen quarterly Roundtables across Australia and New Zealand, covering a broad range of asset classes including;

Mortgages (bank, mutual and non‑bank)

Credit Cards

Personal Loans

Auto & Asset Finance

Commercial Lending

This is an exciting opportunity to join the Perpetual Roundtables team to play a key role in shaping how senior credit risk leaders across Australia and New Zealand benchmark emerging risks, respond to regulatory change, and share best practice.

About the Role

As Principal - Credit & Risk Industry Benchmarking you will be responsible for designing, leading, and delivering industry roundtables and benchmarking initiatives across credit risk, collections, and lending portfolios. The role combines subject matter leadership, external facilitation, analytics expertise, and senior stakeholder engagement, ensuring that member discussions are structured, evidence based, and aligned to emerging risks and industry requirements.

This role acts as the primary point of accountability for roundtable content, member engagement, and the translation of complex regulatory and data topics into practical, peer relevant insights, reporting into the Head of Roundtables.

The role requires some quarterly interstate travel as well as to New Zealand (planned well in advance) to facilitate industry roundtables, engage with stakeholders, and support benchmarking initiatives.

To learn more about the business, you can visit https://www.perpetual.com.au/corporate-trust/perpetual-roundtables/.

Key Responsibilities

Roundtable Leadership & Facilitation

Lead and facilitate multiple industry roundtables both in person and virtually.

Design quarterly agendas that balance structured benchmarking, definitional alignment, live polling, and open member discussion.

Chair meetings, manage time and discussion flow, and ensure all participants are able to contribute meaningfully.

Own the capture of actions, decisions, and insights, and drive documented follow ups through to closure

Member & Stakeholder Engagement

Serve as the primary relationship manager for senior member representatives across major financial institutions.

Project Manager | 12-Month Fixed Term

Brisbane

Are you a seasoned Project Manager with a passion for Lean methodologies and a knack for driving complex, high-impact change? Airbus Australia Pacific is looking for a Project Manager to join our team for a 12-month fixed-term contract.

In this role, you won't just be managing tasks; you'll be an internal consultant delivering quantifiable, bottom-line improvements across our business and functional lines.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Manager of Transformation & Change, you will be at the heart of our Business Improvement (BI) services. You will take ownership of high-profile projects from initiation to closure, applying the Airbus LBIP+ (Lean / DMAIC) methodology to deliver sustainable step-changes in our systems, processes, and organisational structures.

Key responsibilities include:

Managing scope, schedule, budget, and risks for strategically significant projects within a large portfolio.
Lead regulatory, risk, and compliance projects through the full lifecycle, ensuring compliance with standards such as ISO 31000
Applying Lean and Six Sigma tools to ensure project outcomes are both impactful and sustainable.
Engaging with senior leaders and sponsors to ensure project alignment and customer satisfaction.
Supporting our BI Specialists by facilitating workshops and coaching "just-do-it" projects across the business.
What You'll Bring

We are looking for a straightforward, confident communicator who can influence without authority and thrives in a challenging, fast-paced environment.

Essential Criteria:

At least 5 years as a Lead (Project Manager or Work Pack Lead) within a Business Improvement discipline.
Bachelor of Business (or similar).
Project/Program Management certification OR Six Sigma Green/Black Belt.
A track record of achieving quantifiable outcomes and managing complex change.

Desirable Extras:

Lean Practitioner/Coach certification.
Change Management certification.

Why us?

We seek to inspire our people to grow, so you have the right mindset, behaviours and skills to thrive and achieve the organisation goals whilst being focussed on your personal development through classroom and online learning. 

We value the health and wellbeing of our employees and offer true flexibility including an option for working from home that allows you to balance your career with what is important to you outside of work.  We will celebrate your successes and achievements with a formal reward and recognition program.

Female applicants, people of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent and ex-defence personnel are encouraged to apply.

 Application Closing Date: 14 April 2026

About UNSW:

UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. We’re a large organisation with a diverse and talented community; a community doing extraordinary things. Together, we are driven to be thoughtful, practical, and purposeful in all we do. Taking this combined approach is what makes our work matter.
